concept: Hemp / Strong Plant Fiber / Durable Textile
description: A tall plant with strong fibers used for rope and cloth; the workhorse fiber. 'Kenoa' names what holds—the plant that gives strength without breaking.
sound_symbolism: The word stands like the plant itself: tough and upright. 'Ke' begins with velar firmness, the resilience of ancient fiber. 'Noa' grounds with earthy presence. Hemp as durable material, rooted in pre-industrial craft.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'kenoa' (the hemp). Strongest plant fiber. For rope, canvas, durable cloth.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Sustainable strength—fast-growing, needs little water, replaces petroleum products.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Hemp built ships—sails and ropes of the age of exploration.
